title: Kenya Airport Civil War: Why Is the Government Denying the Shelling When Satellite Imagery Shows It
narrative: Kenya's Ministry of Interior and the Civil Aviation Authority characterized the May 7 incident at a regional airport serving a conflict zone as collateral damage from an accidental misidentification of a ground target by government forces. Official statements assert that all civilian infrastructure was spared and that any damage was the result of an isolated targeting error by allied regional forces, not deliberate action. The government has refused international investigators access to the site and stated that an internal inquiry is sufficient. International humanitarian organizations have been denied ground access.
telemetry: SAT-KENYA-01: Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery acquired 2026-05-08 09:14 UTC — circular impact crater 18 meters diameter visible on the main runway threshold, runway-TZ intersection destroyed over a 40-meter stretch, approach lighting infrastructure flattened

SAT-KENYA-02: Planet Labs SkySat stereoscopic imagery 2026-05-08 11:02 UTC — three distinct impact points on the apron area, all within 200 meters of the terminal building — apron surface scorched over 1,400 square meters with thermal anomaly signature consistent with JP-5 jet fuel fire

ENSOR-SEISMIC-01: CTBTO hydroacoustic station HA04 (Indian Ocean) registered seismic event at 2026-05-07 14:23 UTC — magnitude 2.1, depth 0.5 km, location 2.3 km from reported airport — consistent with large conventional ordnance impact

TRANS-AIR-01: ADS-B data from regional air traffic control feeds shows all civilian flight operations into the airport ceased at 14:19 UTC on reporting date — approximately 4 minutes before the seismic event — suggesting pre-knowledge of the impending strike by air traffic control

ENSOR-HUMINT-01: Cross-reference of social media geolocation — 14 verified user posts from airport perimeter with timestamp 14:15-14:21 UTC — all describe low-flying military aircraft in formation before impact, not the single-military-aicraft narrative

FAO-DISPLACEMENT-01: UNHCR displacement tracking matrix shows 847 newly displaced persons from the airport district on May 7-8 — a 340% spike over the 5-day prior average — inconsistent with a narrative of limited collateral damage

SAT-KENYA-03: Maxar Technologies archival imagery from 2026-05-01 — 12 days before incident — confirms the airport was operational with civilian commercial traffic and a humanitarian cargo flight visibly parked on the apron in pre-strike imagery
analysis: The official narrative has three specific claims: (1) accidental misidentification, (2) all civilian infrastructure spared, (3) isolated targeting error. The satellite imagery demolishes all three. A circular impact crater 18 meters in diameter on the runway threshold is not a misidentification — that is the most precisely identifiable target on any runway. The approach lighting infrastructure being flattened is not incidental — those are 30-meter towers with high-visibility globes that are visible from 15 nautical miles in clear conditions. Three distinct impact points within 200 meters of the terminal, with a thermal anomaly consistent with jet fuel fire, is not an isolated error — it is a pattern. The ADS-B data showing civilian operations cessation four minutes before the seismic event is the most damning single data point: the people who manage the airspace knew before the ordnance fell. That is not misidentification — that is coordination. The UNHCR displacement spike, the social media geolocation evidence of multiple low-flying formation aircraft, and the pre-strike commercial operations visible in Maxar archival imagery collectively establish that this was a functioning civilian airport until 14:19 UTC. The government's refusal of international investigators and the internal inquiry offer are the tell: credible governments with credible accidental-misidentification narratives invite ICAO observers. Non-credible ones run internal inquiries.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The official narrative is a post-hoc rationalization — satellite imagery, seismic data, and ADS-B records all show deliberate strikes on a known civilian airport, not an accidental misidentification.
